chunk_summary: This section discusses infant and young child feeding (IYCF) counseling and breastfeeding escalation pathways under India's national nutrition programs, emphasizing tailored interventions, data documentation, and referral systems for feeding challenges.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of IYCF counseling in improving feeding practices and child nutrition outcomes.
Learn the criteria for escalating breastfeeding and feeding concerns to specialized services.
Identify key components of complementary feeding adequacy for children aged 6–23 months.
Explore the integration of local food-based dietary guidelines into feeding practices.
key_facts: POSHAN 2.0 emphasizes IYCF as a convergence outcome delivered through facilities, outreach sessions (VHSND/VHND), and home visits.
Breastfeeding escalation criteria include excessive neonatal weight loss, severe nipple trauma, and preterm/low birthweight feeding complexity.
Complementary feeding adequacy for 6–23 months includes frequency, diversity, texture progression, and responsive feeding.
Growth trajectory interpretation requires at least three weight points for assessment.
Energy-dense, thick complementary feeds using local foods are recommended to improve nutritional intake.
reference_quote: POSHAN 2.0 frames infant and young child feeding (IYCF) as a convergence outcome and emphasizes counseling delivered through facilities, outreach sessions (VHSND/VHND), and home-visit systems.
document_key_topics: Infant and young child feeding counseling
Integration of dietary guidelines into feeding practices
